credit-card.notes: Texas applies a 4-year limitation period to credit card debt as a written contract. The Texas Finance Code Chapter 392 (Texas Debt Collection Act) mirrors and supplements FDCPA protections and covers original creditors as well.
credit-card.citation: Tex. Civ. Prac. & Rem. Code § 16.004; Tex. Fin. Code §§ 392.001–392.404

medical.notes: Medical debts in Texas are subject to the 4-year limitation. Texas has enacted HB 1592 (2023) limiting medical debt reporting to credit bureaus.

auto-loan.notes: Auto loan deficiency balances are subject to Texas's 4-year limitation period from the date the deficiency arises.

personal-loan.notes: Personal loans in Texas are subject to the 4-year limitation period for written debt obligations.

student-loan.notes: Private student loans in Texas follow the 4-year limitation. Federal student loans are exempt from state statutes of limitations for Department of Education collection.

mortgage-deficiency.notes: Mortgage deficiency claims in Texas must be brought within 2 years after the foreclosure sale under the Texas Property Code.

utility-bill.notes: Utility bill debts in Texas are subject to the standard 4-year limitation period.

regulatoryBody: Texas Office of Consumer Credit Commissioner (OCCC) & Texas Attorney General
complaints: File a complaint with the TX OCCC at occc.texas.gov or the TX AG at texasattorneygeneral.gov. Texas's Debt Collection Act (Finance Code Ch. 392) covers all debt collectors including original creditors.
